# evalcheck

> A pytest plugin and GitHub App that posts PR comments for LLM eval regressions. Plain `pytest` invocation; baselines committed to git; no SaaS account required.

## Quickstart

```python
from evalcheck import EvalOutput, eval, faithfulness

@eval(faithfulness, threshold=0.75)
def test_summary():
    return EvalOutput(output=summarise(article), context=article)
```

Run with `pytest`. Results write to `.evalcheck/results.json` automatically. Bless the first run as the baseline with `evalcheck snapshot --update` and commit `.evalcheck/snapshots/baseline.json`. Subsequent runs fail if any score drops below `baseline - 0.05`.

## Built-in metrics

- `exact_match(output, expected) -> float` — deterministic, returns 1.0 or 0.0.
- `regex_match(output, pattern, full_match=False) -> float` — deterministic.
- `faithfulness(output, context, judge=None) -> float` — LLM-as-judge: is the output supported by the context.
- `relevance(output, input, judge=None) -> float` — LLM-as-judge: does the output answer the input.
- `correctness(output, expected, judge=None) -> float` — LLM-as-judge with semantic equivalence.
- `custom(fn)` — pass any `Callable[..., float]` returning [0, 1].

## Judge providers

`OpenAIJudge` and `AnthropicJudge` ship out of the box. Configurable via `EVALCHECK_JUDGE_MODEL` (e.g. `openai:gpt-4o-mini`, `anthropic:claude-haiku-4-5`). Judge model uses `temperature=0` for determinism.

## File: pyproject.toml

```toml
[project]
dependencies = ["evalcheck"]
# or [project.optional-dependencies]: ["evalcheck[anthropic]"], etc.
```

## Configuration

The decorator is the configuration surface. There is intentionally no global `[tool.evalcheck]` config in v0.1.

```python
@eval(
    metric,                       # callable returning float in [0, 1]
    threshold=0.7,                # fail if score below this
    regression_tolerance=0.05,    # fail if score drops > this below baseline
    baseline_path=None,           # default: .evalcheck/snapshots/baseline.json
    test_id=None,                 # default: PYTEST_CURRENT_TEST
    **metric_kwargs,              # forwarded to the metric (e.g. pattern, judge)
)
```

## Snapshot schema

`.evalcheck/snapshots/baseline.json` and `.evalcheck/results.json` share this shape:

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "runs": [
    {
      "test_id": "tests/test_x.py::test_a",
      "metric": "faithfulness",
      "score": 0.84,
      "threshold": 0.7,
      "timestamp": "2026-04-29T10:00:00+00:00"
    }
  ]
}
```

## CLI

- `evalcheck snapshot --update` — bless `.evalcheck/results.json` as the new baseline.
- `evalcheck snapshot --update --results <path> --baseline <path>` — custom paths.

## What evalcheck does NOT do

- A web playground, dashboard, or hosted service (the OSS plugin is git-only).
- Cost or latency tracking (use observability tools).
- Dataset generation.
- Model fine-tuning suggestions.

## Repository layout

- `evalcheck/decorator.py` — the `@eval` decorator wrapper.
- `evalcheck/plugin.py` — pytest hooks (`pytest_sessionstart`, `pytest_sessionfinish`).
- `evalcheck/judge.py` — provider abstraction.
- `evalcheck/metrics/` — metric implementations.
- `evalcheck/snapshot.py` — JSON read/write.
- `evalcheck/cli.py` — `evalcheck` command.

## Source

- Plugin: https://github.com/Boiga7/evalcheck
- GitHub App (paired): https://github.com/Boiga7/evalcheck-app
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/evalcheck/
- Comparisons: https://github.com/Boiga7/evalcheck/tree/main/docs/comparisons
